text.title: AI IaC Red-Flags Output Contract
text.summary: Pins the red-flag report shape, severity scheme, and PR-comment structure.

section.title: Required redflag-report fields

list.item: pr_branch (string)
catalogue_version (string; matches templates/redflag-catalogue.md version)
iac_files (array of repo-relative paths)
flags (array of objects, one per detected flag)
flags[].id (one of RF-01..RF-08)
flags[].file + flags[].line_range
flags[].snippet (the offending text)
flags[].provider_schema_ref (URL or null; required for RF-01 / RF-02)
flags[].severity (enum: block, advise)
flags[].recommendation (concrete fix; not vague advice)
recommendation (enum: ship, revise, escalate)

section.reference: Authoritative JSON Schema for the red-flag report.

list.item: block on every RF-01, RF-02, RF-04 (secret), RF-06 (dangling output).
advise by default on RF-03 (version drift), RF-05 (for_each misuse), RF-07 (shape mismatch), RF-08 (tag).
Severity is upgraded to `block` for RF-08 if tag-policy.yaml marks the missing tag as required.
One section per flag, ordered by severity then line number.
Each section: header `RF-NN: ` + file:line + snippet block + schema reference + recommendation block.
Summary block at top: count by severity, total flags, recommendation.

antipattern.description: RF-01 / RF-02 flagged without provider_schema_ref URL.
antipattern.reason: r2 violation. Schema is authoritative; the reference proves the flag is real.
Secrets MUST be scrubbed from history; the recommendation MUST list the scrubbing steps.

antipattern.description: Report recommendation `ship` while flags list has any `block` severity entry.
antipattern.reason: Decision must follow the severity scheme.

list.item: catalogue_version matches the file on disk.
Every flag has a recommendation string.
RF-01 / RF-02 flags include a schema URL.
RF-04 flags include scrubbing steps.
Top-level recommendation derives from flag severities, not from independent judgement.
